InjectableMetric.description: Injectable sensor metrics — the measured subset of the MQTT sensor-reading metric enum, kept in sync with it. The derived `vpd` is deliberately excluded: it is not a channel, so reach it by injecting temperature + humidity (controller HAL §9, sensing §3).

SensorInjectionPut.description: Request to inject a sensor reading on the simulated HAL (controller HAL §9). The metric is taken from the path; this body carries the forced value, an optional probe/zone selector, and an optional auto-expiry. Simulated backend only — a real-hardware backend rejects these paths with 404.

value.description: Forced reading, in the metric's fixed unit (temperature °C, humidity %RH, co2 ppm, par µmol·m⁻²·s⁻¹, soil_moisture VWC 0–1). Out-of-plausibility values are controller-enforced and surface as 422.

probe_index.description: metric=temperature only: which redundant probe to force (0-based). Omit/null to force all probes — driving the fused median (e.g. to trip the critical-temperature interlock); set it to force one probe — driving outlier/disagreement detection. Non-null on any other metric is rejected with 422.

zone_id.description: metric=soil_moisture only: the target zone; null/absent for house-level metrics. Required for soil_moisture, where its absence is rejected with 422.

ttl_secs.description: Seconds until the injection auto-expires; omit or null to use the controller's configured default (sensor_injection_timeout_secs, controller config §).

SensorInjection.description: An active sensor-reading injection on the simulated HAL (controller HAL §9).

value.description: Forced reading in the metric's fixed unit.

probe_index.description: Forced temperature probe (0-based), or null when all probes are forced / the metric is not temperature.

zone_id.description: Target zone for soil_moisture; null for house-level metrics.

created_at.description: When the injection was set (RFC 3339 UTC, ms).

expires_at.description: When the injection auto-expires (RFC 3339 UTC, ms), or null for no timeout.

TimeScalePut.description: Request to set the simulated clock's time-scale (speed) on the simulated HAL (controller HAL §7, architecture §3). `scale` multiplies the wall-clock tick cadence only (sleep = tick_period_ms / scale); it does NOT change the per-tick simulation step Δt, so determinism is preserved. Latched to the next tick like every other write. Simulated backend only — a real-hardware backend rejects this with 404.

scale.description: Wall-clock cadence multiplier. 1.0 = real-time; <1 slows down (0.5 = half speed); >1 speeds up (2.0, 4.0 = fast-forward). Canonical UI stops are 0.5/1/2/4, but any value in the range is accepted. The 8.0 ceiling leaves headroom above the per-tick compute budget (`P1-PERF-3`, ≤100 ms): at scale S the wall interval is 1000/S ms, so 8× → 125 ms. Out-of-range values are rejected with 422.

TimeScale.description: The simulated clock's current time-scale state on the simulated HAL (controller HAL §7). Ephemeral runtime state — resets to the configured default (1.0) on restart, like manual overrides and sensor injections.

tick_index.description: Monotonic simulation tick counter since startup. One tick = one simulated second (Δt), independent of `scale`.

updated_at.description: When the scale was last set, as the controller's clock instant (RFC 3339 UTC, ms).
